description: Martingale theorems from Saporito Ch 2 — routed to Lean (Mathlib)

theorems.name: Doob Decomposition (Existence and Uniqueness)
theorems.description: Every adapted integrable process f decomposes as f = M + A with M a martingale and A predictable null at 0, uniquely up to a.e. equality at every time. The existence-plus-uniqueness packaging is assembled in MathFin from Mathlib's martingalePart/predictablePart lemmas.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.DoobDecomposition

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

theorem doob_decomposition_thm {Ω E : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
    {μ : Measure Ω} {ℱ : Filtration ℕ m0} [SigmaFiniteFiltration μ ℱ]
    {f : ℕ → Ω → E} (hf : StronglyAdapted ℱ f) (hf_int : ∀ n, Integrable (f n) μ) :
    ∃ M A : ℕ → Ω → E,
      (Martingale M ℱ μ ∧ (StronglyAdapted ℱ fun n => A (n + 1)) ∧ A 0 = 0 ∧
        M + A = f) ∧
      ∀ M' A' : ℕ → Ω → E, Martingale M' ℱ μ →
        (StronglyAdapted ℱ fun n => A' (n + 1)) → A' 0 = 0 →
        (∀ n, Integrable (A' n) μ) → M' + A' = f →
        ∀ n, M' n =ᵐ[μ] M n ∧ A' n =ᵐ[μ] A n :=
  MathFin.doob_decomposition hf hf_int

metadata.formalization_scope: Full textbook statement: existence AND a.e.-uniqueness of the decomposition. Mathlib provides all component lemmas (martingalePart/predictablePart, their martingale/predictability/zero facts, and the two essential-uniqueness lemmas martingalePart_add_ae_eq / predictablePart_add_ae_eq); this library's contribution (MathFin.Foundations.DoobDecomposition) is the single existence-plus-uniqueness packaging, which Mathlib never states. Previous entry stated only the near-definitional sum identity. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Optional Sampling Inequality (bounded times, submartingale)
theorems.description: Conditional-expectation-form optional sampling for submartingales: for stopping times σ ≤ τ bounded by n, stoppedValue f σ ≤ᵐ μ[stoppedValue f τ | ℱ_σ] (MathFin.submartingale_optional_sampling) — derived by Doob decomposition: the martingale part transports across stopping times by Mathlib's optional-sampling equality, and the compensator is a.s. monotone (exactly the submartingale property). Strictly stronger than the expectation form E[f_σ] ≤ E[f_τ] (Submartingale.expected_stoppedValue_mono), which is all Mathlib provides for submartingales.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.OptionalSamplingInequality

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

theorem submartingale_optional_sampling_thm
    {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure Ω}
    {ℱ : MeasureTheory.Filtration ℕ m0} {f : ℕ → Ω → ℝ} {τ σ : Ω → ℕ∞} {n : ℕ}
    [MeasureTheory.SigmaFiniteFiltration μ ℱ]
    (hf : MeasureTheory.Submartingale f ℱ μ)
    (hτ : MeasureTheory.IsStoppingTime ℱ τ) (hσ : MeasureTheory.IsStoppingTime ℱ σ)
    (hσ_le_τ : σ ≤ τ) (hτ_le : ∀ ω, τ ω ≤ n)
    [MeasureTheory.SigmaFinite (μ.trim hσ.measurableSpace_le)] :
    MeasureTheory.stoppedValue f σ ≤ᵐ[μ]
      μ[MeasureTheory.stoppedValue f τ | hσ.measurableSpace] :=
  MathFin.submartingale_optional_sampling hf hτ hσ hσ_le_τ hτ_le

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation (upgraded-properly 2026-06-04): the conditional-expectation-form optional sampling INEQUALITY for submartingales, stoppedValue f sigma <=ae mu[stoppedValue f tau | F_sigma] for bounded stopping times sigma <= tau <= n (Foundations/OptionalSamplingInequality.lean) — absent from Mathlib at the pin (which has the martingale EQUALITY, Martingale.stoppedValue_ae_eq_condExp_of_le, and only the expectation-form submartingale inequality). Derivation = Doob decomposition: the martingale part transports by Mathlib's optional sampling equality; the compensator predictablePart is a.s. nondecreasing (its increments are mu[f_{k+1} - f_k | F_k] >= 0, exactly the submartingale property), so its stopped values compare pathwise and condExp_mono transports the comparison. Replaces the former wrapper around the expectation-form Submartingale.expected_stoppedValue_mono. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Doob Maximal Inequality
theorems.description: Theorem 2.4.3: For a non-negative submartingale, λ P(max_{k≤n} X_k ≥ λ) ≤ E[X_n]

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.Martingale.OptionalStopping

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter

-- Doob's maximal inequality (Mathlib's `MeasureTheory.maximal_ineq`).
-- For a non-negative submartingale f and any ε ≥ 0,
--   ε · μ{ω | ε ≤ max_{k≤n} f k ω} ≤ ∫_{ε≤f*_n} f n dμ.
example {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω}
    [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {𝒢 : Filtration ℕ m0} {f : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}
    (hsub : Submartingale f 𝒢 μ) (hnonneg : 0 ≤ f) (ε : NNReal) (n : ℕ) :
    ε • μ {ω | (ε : ℝ) ≤ (Finset.range (n + 1)).sup' Finset.nonempty_range_add_one
                            (fun k => f k ω)} ≤
    ENNReal.ofReal (∫ ω in {ω | (ε : ℝ) ≤ (Finset.range (n + 1)).sup'
      Finset.nonempty_range_add_one (fun k => f k ω)}, f n ω ∂μ) :=
  maximal_ineq hsub hnonneg n

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: L²-bounded Martingale Convergence (a.s. and L²)
theorems.description: A martingale bounded in L² converges to the limit process almost surely AND in L²-norm. The L² half is absent from Mathlib; it is proved in MathFin via the library's Doob L² maximal inequality (envelope dominator), Chebyshev, and Vitali's convergence theorem.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.L2MartingaleConvergence

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

theorem martingale_L2_convergence_thm {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    {μ : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure μ] {ℱ : Filtration ℕ m0}
    {f : ℕ → Ω → ℝ} {R : ℝ≥0}
    (hf : Martingale f ℱ μ) (hbdd : ∀ n, eLpNorm (f n) 2 μ ≤ R) :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, Filter.Tendsto (fun n => f n ω) Filter.atTop
      (nhds (ℱ.limitProcess f μ ω))) ∧
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => eLpNorm (f n - ℱ.limitProcess f μ) 2 μ)
      Filter.atTop (nhds 0) :=
  MathFin.martingale_ae_tendsto_and_eLpNorm_two_tendsto hf hbdd

metadata.formalization_scope: Full statement: a.e. convergence (Mathlib's upcrossing-based ae_tendsto_limitProcess) AND L²-norm convergence. The L² half is NOT in Mathlib; MathFin.Foundations.L2MartingaleConvergence derives it by manufacturing uniform integrability in L² from this library's Doob L² maximal inequality (DoobLpMaximalInequality.eLpNorm_norm_runMax_le: monotone-convergence envelope dominator + Chebyshev + MemLp.eLpNorm_indicator_le), closed by Vitali. Differentiates this entry from mart-thm-2.5.3 (the a.s.-only Mathlib wrapper). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Upcrossing Inequality
theorems.description: Proposition 2.5.5: E[U_n([a,b])] ≤ E[(X_n - a)⁻] / (b - a) where U_n counts upcrossings of [a,b]

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.Martingale.Upcrossing

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

-- Mathlib's `MeasureTheory.upcrossingsBefore a b f N` counts the upcrossings of [a, b]
-- by the process f before time N. The submartingale upcrossing inequality is exactly
-- `Submartingale.mul_integral_upcrossingsBefore_le_integral_pos_part`:
--   (b - a) · E[upcrossingsBefore a b f N] ≤ E[(f N - a)⁺].
example {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {ℱ : Filtration ℕ m0} {f : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}
    (a b : ℝ) (hf : Submartingale f ℱ μ) (N : ℕ) :
    (b - a) * μ[fun ω => (MeasureTheory.upcrossingsBefore a b f N ω : ℝ)]
      ≤ μ[fun ω => (f N ω - a)⁺] :=
  hf.mul_integral_upcrossingsBefore_le_integral_pos_part a b N

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Martingale Transform
theorems.description: Theorem 2.2.9: If M is a martingale and A is non-anticipative (predictable, a.e. uniformly bounded), then (A·M)_n = sum_{k=1}^n A_k (M_k - M_{k-1}) is a martingale

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.MartingaleTransform

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Theorem 2.2.9: the discrete-time martingale transform of a martingale `M`
    by an a.e.-uniformly-bounded predictable process `A` is itself a
    martingale (the a.e. bound is weaker than the textbook pointwise bound). Re-export of
    `MathFin.martingaleTransform_isMartingale` (real derivation in
    `MathFin/Foundations/MartingaleTransform.lean`, derived from Mathlib
    `condExp_add` / `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` / `martingale_nat`).
    The proof lives in a Lake-built library so each file gets the full
    incremental-compilation memory budget; the benchmark snippet only
    typechecks against the prebuilt library. -/
theorem martingale_transform_is_martingale
    {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    {μ : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure μ] {𝓕 : Filtration ℕ m0}
    {M A : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}
    (hM : Martingale M 𝓕 μ)
    (hA : StronglyAdapted 𝓕 (fun n => A (n + 1)))
    (hA_bdd : ∃ K : ℝ, ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, ∀ n, |A n ω| ≤ K) :
    Martingale (MathFin.martingaleTransform A M) 𝓕 μ :=
  MathFin.martingaleTransform_isMartingale hM hA hA_bdd

metadata.formalization_scope: Real Lean derivation of Theorem 2.2.9: re-export of MathFin.martingaleTransform_isMartingale (MathFin/Foundations/MartingaleTransform.lean). The boundedness hypothesis is the a.e. form (∃ K, ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, ∀ n, |A n ω| ≤ K) — weaker than the textbook's pointwise bound, so the statement is strictly stronger. Proof: the martingale_of_condExp_sub_eq_zero_nat characterization; the transform increment A_{n+1}(M_{n+1} − M_n) has vanishing conditional expectation via condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left (pull-out) + the martingale increment property of M; integrability of each summand from the a.e. bound. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Doob's L^p Inequality
theorems.description: Theorem 2.4.6: For p > 1 and a non-negative submartingale (M_n), ||M_n*||_p ≤ (p/(p-1)) ||M_n||_p where M_n* = max_{k≤n} M_k

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.DoobLpMaximalInequality

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Theorem 2.4.6 (Doob's L^p maximal inequality, full formal proof).
    For `p > 1` and a non-negative submartingale `M`, the L^p norm of the
    running maximum `max_{k ≤ n} M_k` is bounded by `(p / (p - 1))` times
    the L^p norm of `M n`.

    Direct re-export of `MeasureTheory.maximal_ineq_Lp`, proved end-to-end
    from first principles via layer cake + Doob's L^1 maximal inequality +
    Fubini (Tonelli) swap + Hölder + rpow inversion + truncation/monotone
    convergence. Axioms-clean (`#print axioms` confirms
    `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). -/
theorem doob_lp_maximal_inequality
    {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℕ m0} {M : ℕ → Ω → ℝ} {p : ℝ}
    (hsub : Submartingale M 𝓕 μ) (hnn : ∀ n ω, 0 ≤ M n ω)
    (hp : 1 < p) (n : ℕ) :
    eLpNorm (fun ω ↦ (Finset.range (n + 1)).sup' Finset.nonempty_range_add_one
        (fun k ↦ M k ω)) (ENNReal.ofReal p) μ
      ≤ ENNReal.ofReal (p / (p - 1)) *
          eLpNorm (M n) (ENNReal.ofReal p) μ :=
  MeasureTheory.maximal_ineq_Lp hsub hnn hp n

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of Doob's L^p maximal inequality. End-to-end derivation (no sorries, no library wrapper) from Mathlib primitives: layer cake (MeasureTheory.lintegral_rpow_eq_lintegral_meas_le_mul) + Doob's L^1 maximal inequality (MeasureTheory.maximal_ineq) + Fubini/Tonelli swap (MeasureTheory.lintegral_lintegral_swap) + Hölder inequality (ENNReal.lintegral_mul_le_Lp_mul_Lq with HolderConjugate p (p/(p-1))) + rpow inversion (ENNReal.rpow_add_of_nonneg + ENNReal.div_le_iff) + truncation argument (min(runMax, K) + monotone convergence via lintegral_iSup) for the A=infty, B<infty corner. Lives at MathFin/Foundations/DoobLpMaximalInequality.lean (18 helper lemmas + MeasureTheory.maximal_ineq_Lp). Axioms-clean: depends only on propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

v430_recovery.note: Promoted to full after completing end-to-end Doob L^p proof in MathFin/Foundations/DoobLpMaximalInequality.lean (Fubini swap + Holder + truncation + monotone convergence). #print axioms clean.

theorems.name: L¹ Martingale Convergence
theorems.description: An L1-bounded submartingale converges almost surely to limitProcess (Mathlib Submartingale.ae_tendsto_limitProcess). Integrability of the limit is not separately wrapped here.

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.Martingale.Convergence

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Filter

-- Direct wrapper: Mathlib's L1-bounded submartingale a.s. convergence theorem.
example {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω}
    [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {ℱ : Filtration ℕ m0} {f : ℕ → Ω → ℝ} {R : NNReal}
    (hf : Submartingale f ℱ μ)
    (hbdd : ∀ n, eLpNorm (f n) 1 μ ≤ R) :
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂μ, Tendsto (fun n => f n ω) atTop (nhds (ℱ.limitProcess f μ ω)) :=
  hf.ae_tendsto_limitProcess hbdd

metadata.formalization_scope: library_wrapper of the a.s.-convergence theorem; faithful for the a.s. claim.

theorems.name: First Fundamental Theorem of Asset Pricing (1st part)
theorems.description: Theorem 2.6.7: If there exists an equivalent martingale measure Q, then the discrete-time market is arbitrage-free

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.FTAP

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Theorem 2.6.7 (FTAP, ⇒ direction): existence of an equivalent martingale
    measure precludes arbitrage. Re-export of
    `MathFin.emm_implies_no_arbitrage` (real derivation in
    `MathFin/Foundations/FTAP.lean`, applies the martingale-transform theorem
    to discounted prices under the EMM `Q`, then uses `Q ≪ P` and
    `integral_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg_ae`). The martingale-transform helper is
    shared with `mart-thm-2.2.9` via `import MathFin.Foundations.MartingaleTransform`
    rather than duplicated inline. -/
theorem emm_implies_no_arbitrage
    {Ω : Type*} {m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {T : ℕ} {𝓕 : Filtration ℕ m0} {S : ℕ → Ω → ℝ}
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    (hQP : Q ≪ P) (hPQ : P ≪ Q)
    (hSQ : Martingale S 𝓕 Q)
    (φ : ℕ → Ω → ℝ)
    (hφ_pred : StronglyAdapted 𝓕 (fun n => φ (n + 1)))
    (hφ_bdd : ∃ K : ℝ, ∀ n ω, |φ n ω| ≤ K)
    (hV_nonneg : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P,
      0 ≤ ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, φ (t + 1) ω * (S (t + 1) ω - S t ω)) :
    P {ω | 0 < ∑ t ∈ Finset.range T, φ (t + 1) ω * (S (t + 1) ω - S t ω)} = 0 :=
  MathFin.emm_implies_no_arbitrage hQP hPQ hSQ φ hφ_pred hφ_bdd hV_nonneg

metadata.formalization_scope: Real derivation of FTAP (⇒ direction): embeds the martingale-transform theorem (mart-thm-2.2.9 inlined as helper since each benchmark is verified in isolation), applies it to the discounted price `S` under the EMM `Q`, then uses `Q ≪ P`, `integral_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg_ae`, and `P ≪ Q` to derive that arbitrage sets have `P`-measure zero.
